response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As someone who has been using social media for a while now, I have noticed that there is a lot of fake content floating around. From fake news to fake profiles, it can be difficult to know what to believe anymore. However, there are some things you can do to manage and deal with fake content on social media. 
Firstly, it's important to be aware of the source of the content you are consuming. Don't just take everything at face value, especially if it seems too good (or bad) to be true. Do your research and verify the credibility of the source before sharing or believing the information. 
Secondly, be cautious when interacting with new profiles or accounts on social media. If a profile seems suspicious or doesn't have any connections or followers, it could be a fake account created to spread misinformation or scam people. 
Thirdly, use fact-checking websites and tools to help you identify and flag fake content on social media. These tools can help you verify the accuracy of information and report suspicious activity to the platform. 
Lastly, it's important to be mindful of your own online presence and reputation. Make sure that the information you post is accurate and respectful, and avoid engaging in behaviors that could harm your reputation or lead to misunderstandings with others. By being aware of these tips, you can help manage and deal with fake content on social media more effectively.
